title: teucrium × ochroleucum var. gautieri
intro: Teucrium × ochroleucum var. gautieri, also known as alpine germander, is a hybrid plant species of the Teucrium genus, commonly found in mountainous regions of Europe. It possesses medicinal properties, particularly in its essential oils, which are known for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of alpine germander include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a useful remedy for various ailments. Alpine germander can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
